title: Monte Carlo Tree Search with Policy-Based Pruning
summary: This paper introduces a novel pruning technique for MCTS using a lightweight policy network to skip unpromising branches early.

key_claims.claim: Policy-based pruning reduces search time by 40% with minimal impact on decision quality.

methods.name: Policy-Guided Branch Pruning
methods.description: Uses a small MLP to predict branch value and prune if below a threshold.

limitations: May prune optimal branches in extremely sparse reward environments.

suggested_followups.description: Implement policy-guided pruning in the IX MCTS engine.
suggested_followups.rationale: High applicability to IX and significant potential speedup.

issue_draft.title: [IX] Implement Policy-Guided MCTS Pruning
issue_draft.body:
Based on digest-2024-001, implement the pruning technique to improve search efficiency.

- [ ] Port MLP evaluator to Rust
- [ ] Integrate with MCTS loop
- [ ] Benchmark against baseline
